title: stapelia glauca
intro: Stapelia glauca, also known as the carrion flower or ghost flower, is a unique, succulent plant species that blooms with a strong, unpleasant odor to attract carrion-feeding flies for pollination. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor effects. The health benefits of Stapelia glauca are thought to include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and potentially inhibiting the growth of cancer cells. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying the flowers and leaves, which can then be used in teas, infusions, or as a dried powder for medicinal purposes.
